query: I'm using ODIN for OOD detection. I picked the temperature T and the input perturbation epsilon by sweeping values and selecting the pair that maximized AUROC on my OOD test set. AUROC is 0.94. Is that good?
expected_behavior: Identifies the hyperparameter-tuning-on-the-test-OOD-set as a leakage / overfitting concern that makes the 0.94 AUROC optimistically biased

Recommends a leakage-free alternative — e.g., splitting the OOD data into a tune half and a held-out test half, using the ODIN paper defaults, or switching to a method without OOD-set hyperparameters (energy, Mahalanobis)
Does NOT accept the 0.94 number at face value as the deployment-quality estimate without a leakage-corrected re-evaluation
